title: Colombia's President Visits Venezuela After Maduro's Abduction
summary: Have you ever had a friend who suddenly disappeared? That's kind of what happened to Venezuela's former president, Nicolas Maduro. In January, the US military took him away (some call it an abduction) and he's now waiting for trial in the US. Since then, no foreign leader had visited Venezuela—until now. Colombia's President Gustavo Petro went to Caracas and met with Venezuela's interim president, Delcy Rodriguez. They hugged and waved before going inside the presidential palace. The two leaders talked about security along their shared border, which is 2,200 kilometers long. That border is important for trade, but it's also used by criminals who smuggle drugs and people. Petro has been a strong critic of the US for taking Maduro. He called it an "assault on sovereignty" (an attack on a country's independence). The US says it was a law enforcement operation. Rodriguez has been walking a careful line. She has agreed to some US demands, like stopping oil exports to Cuba and releasing political prisoners, but she also wants the US to lift sanctions (punishments) that hurt Venezuela's economy. She even accepted an invitation to meet US President Trump, but no date is set yet. This visit is a big step for Venezuela as it tries to fix its economic crisis.
why_it_matters: This story shows how countries can talk to each other even after big problems, and how what happens in one country can affect its neighbors — just like when you and a friend work things out after a disagreement.

keywords.explanation: Gustavo Petro, the leader of Colombia.
A country in South America next to Colombia.
The former president of Venezuela who was taken by the US military.
A temporary leader, in this case Delcy Rodriguez.
The line between Colombia and Venezuela that is 2,200 kilometers long.
Economic punishments that one country places on another to change its behavior.

questions.question: Who became the first foreign leader to visit Venezuela since Maduro was taken?
Why was Nicolas Maduro taken to the US?
options: He was invited to a conference

questions.question: What did Petro and Rodriguez discuss?
What did Petro call the US taking Maduro?
Which country does Delcy Rodriguez now lead as interim president?
What does Venezuela want the US to lift?

background_read: Venezuela used to be one of the richest countries in South America because of its oil.
In 2025, the US military captured former President Nicolas Maduro and brought him to the US to face trial.
Neighboring countries often worry about the border because criminals use it to smuggle drugs and people.
Article_Structure: WHO: Gustavo Petro (Colombia's president), Delcy Rodriguez (Venezuela's interim president), Nicolas Maduro (former president)
WHAT: Petro visits Venezuela for the first time since Maduro's abduction; they discuss border security and sanctions
WHERE: Caracas, the capital of Venezuela, at the Miraflores Presidential Palace
WHY: To improve relations between the two countries and address border issues; Rodriguez wants the US to lift sanctions

perspectives.perspective: Venezuelan citizens who support the new government
perspectives.description: They hope the visit leads to better trade and more help from Colombia, and maybe even an end to economic sanctions.
They see the visit as a chance for Venezuela to cooperate more with the US, but they want to keep pressure on until more changes happen.
They are watching closely to see if the new government will really free political prisoners and hold fair elections.
